name: Kid Ory
abstract: Edward "Kid" Ory (December 25, 1886 – January 23, 1973) was an American jazz composer, trombonist and bandleader. One of the early users of the glissando technique, he helped establish it as a central element of New Orleans jazz.

concerts.label: Kid Ory and His Creole Jazz Band

works.t: Bill Bailey, Won't You Please Come Home?
I'm Busy and You Can't Come In

about_quotes.speaker: Peter Vacher
about_quotes.evidence: By that time I knew who Kid Ory was and I was aware that that was the kind of jazz music I wanted to have a go at.
about_quotes.doc_title: Peter Vacher interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 18, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Albert Burbank
about_quotes.evidence: I didn't know him until, until he sent for me. And I went out in California and played with him for three months.

about_quotes.speaker: R.C.H. Smith
about_quotes.evidence: I did play in his band on his pickup gigs and on weekends during the period of about 1958 through 1960.

about_quotes.speaker: Avakian George
about_quotes.evidence: I did an album with Kid Ory with the help of Nesuhi Ertegun... I met Ory in an unusual way. On that trip in 1939 to California... that’s when I met Kid Ory.
about_quotes.doc_title: Avakian George — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Lionel Reason
about_quotes.evidence: And I went to work with Ory... Ory hummed whole arrangements to me... And I make the arrangements for them. Then we recorded for Good Times Yes.

about_quotes.speaker: Andrew Blakeney
about_quotes.evidence: after I started working with Ory... My work with Ory was playing casuals, clubs, movies and recordings.
